title: Chinese Tech Giant Baidu is Launching its Own Version of the Metaverse; Here’s How
summary: Baidu, China's largest search engine, is developing its own metaverse platform called XiRang, which formally launched last week, though a full rollout could take over six years. The platform allows users to interact in a shared online space with personalized avatars and virtual attractions, supporting up to 100,000 users and accessible only to Chinese developers currently. Despite the ambitious plans and a high demand for online interaction in China, regulatory hurdles, particularly regarding digital assets, mean Baidu's metaverse will not support cryptocurrencies.

title: Baidu to launch Level 2 autonomous car in 2023
summary: Baidu, the Chinese internet and AI giant, plans to launch a Level-2 autonomous electric vehicle in 2023 through its joint venture with Geely, Jidu Auto. The vehicle will feature self-driving technology powered by Nvidia chips and aims to capitalize on the growing global demand for EVs. This move positions Baidu among other tech companies, including Huawei, Alibaba, Tencent, and Apple, entering the EV market.

title: BAIDU, INC. (NASDAQ: BIDU) SHAREHOLDER CLASS ACTION ALERT:
summary: Bernstein Liebhard LLP has announced a securities class action lawsuit against Baidu Inc. (NASDAQ: BIDU) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between March 22, 2021, and March 29, 2021. The lawsuit alleges that Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and Morgan Stanley unlawfully traded Baidu shares using non-public information related to Archegos Capital Management's margin call failure, causing losses for Baidu investors. Investors are invited to join the lawsuit, with a lead plaintiff deadline of February 14, 2022.

title: China’s autonomous driving enters fast lane, boosted by NEV growth and favorable policies
summary: China's autonomous driving sector is rapidly advancing due to robust new energy vehicle (NEV) growth and supportive government policies, despite the COVID-19 pandemic. Cities like Shanghai are implementing measures to commercialize self-driving technology, including pilot programs for Robotaxi services and legislative support for operations without in-car safety operators. Chinese companies are not only accelerating commercialization domestically, but also expanding into overseas markets, positioning China to potentially lead the global autonomous driving industry.

title: FINAL DEADLINE MONDAY: ROSEN, GLOBAL INVESTOR COUNSEL,
summary: The Rosen Law Firm is reminding investors who purchased Baidu Inc. (NASDAQ: BIDU) securities between March 22, 2021, and March 29, 2021, of the February 14, 2022, lead plaintiff deadline for a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley illegally sold a large number of Baidu shares during the Class Period while possessing material, non-public information regarding Archegos Capital Management's forced liquidation of its Baidu position, causing significant losses for other investors. Investors with losses are encouraged to secure counsel, with the Rosen Law Firm highlighting its track record in securities class actions.

title: Baidu Brings Apollo Go Robotaxi Service to Downtown Shenzhen, Expanding Presence to All First-tier Cities in China
summary: Baidu's Apollo Go robotaxi service has launched trial operations in Shenzhen's Nanshan District, making Shenzhen the seventh city in China to offer the service. This expansion marks Baidu's presence in all first-tier cities in China and highlights its goal to accelerate the development of intelligent transportation. Baidu aims to expand Apollo Go operations to 65 cities by 2025 and 100 cities by 2030, offering autonomous ride-hailing to a wider user base.

title: China’s Baidu Launches Self-Driving Taxis in Densely Populated Shenzhen
summary: Baidu has launched its self-driving taxi service, Apollo Go, in Shenzhen, marking its first deployment in a densely populated Chinese urban area. The service uses 50 pick-up and drop-off stations and allows users to hail vehicles via a smartphone app. While human drivers are currently present for safety, Baidu plans significant expansion, aiming for 65 cities by 2025 and 100 by 2030, putting China at the forefront of autonomous vehicle development.

title: Baidu Stock Jumps Even as Earnings Miss Estimates. Here’s What to Like.
summary: Baidu's stock jumped despite missing earnings estimates, driven by growth in its cloud computing and artificial intelligence segments. This growth is helping the Chinese tech giant navigate a slowdown in its core business. The company, like others in the Chinese tech sector, is facing macroeconomic headwinds.

title: Infographic: A snapshot of Baidu’s (BIDU) Q4 2021 earnings
summary: Baidu Inc. reported lower earnings for the fourth quarter of 2021, despite a 9% increase in revenues to RMB33.1 billion ($5.2 billion). The revenue growth was driven by its non-advertising business, particularly the Baidu AI Cloud. However, adjusted earnings declined to RMB11.60 per ADS ($1.82 per ADS) compared to the prior year.

title: Baidu Launches AI Platform to Enable on-Device, Real-Time Translation from Speech to Hand Gestures
summary: Baidu AI Cloud has launched a new AI sign language platform, XiLing, capable of generating digital avatars for real-time sign language translation and live interpretation. This platform, along with two all-in-one translators, aims to bridge the communication gap for the deaf and hard-of-hearing community in China, which faces a severe shortage of human sign language interpreters. The technology addresses complexities in sign language translation, including speech recognition clarity, translation accuracy, and fluency of sign language movements, enabling new accessibility for DHH individuals in various online and offline scenarios.

title: Baidu to launch self-driving taxi service in Zhejiang
summary: Baidu Inc. is launching a self-driving taxi service, Apollo Go, in Wuzhen, Zhejiang province, on Saturday. This initiative is part of Baidu's broader strategy to commercialize autonomous driving technology. Apollo Go has 84 pick-up and drop-off stations, and Baidu has already provided 213,000 autonomous rides in Q4 2021, positioning it as a leading autonomous mobility service provider globally.

title: Baidu Proposes PP-YOLOE: An Evolved Version of YOLO that Achieves SOTA Performance in Object Detection
summary: Baidu researchers have introduced PP-YOLOE, an evolved version of the YOLO object detector, which demonstrates state-of-the-art performance in object detection. This new model surpasses previous versions like YOLOX and PP-YOLOv2 in both speed and accuracy on COCO datasets. The improvements stem from architectural optimizations, an anchor-free method, novel RepResBlock usage, task alignment learning, and an Efficient Task-aligned Head.

title: China ends nine-month freeze on game approvals
summary: China has ended its nine-month freeze on game approvals, issuing licenses for 45 new games. This marks a significant development following a period of strict regulatory crackdowns on the gaming industry, including limits on gaming time for minors. While thousands of game developers shut down in 2021, the article notes that this trend has been ongoing and not solely attributable to the recent freeze.

title: Baidu expands self-driving taxi service to Zhejiang’s Wuzhen
summary: Chinese tech giant Baidu Inc. is expanding its self-driving taxi service, Apollo Go, to Wuzhen, Zhejiang Province. This initiative aims to accelerate the large-scale commercialization of autonomous driving technology. Baidu's autonomous ride-hailing service provided approximately 213,000 rides in Q4 2021, making it the world's largest autonomous mobility service provider.

title: Beijing grants Baidu, Pony.ai new driverless robotaxi permits
summary: Baidu and Pony.ai have received permits from Beijing to deploy robotaxis without human safety drivers on open roads for the first time. This approval allows them to offer rides to the public in a 60 square kilometer area in the Beijing Economic and Technological Development Zone. While Baidu will fully deploy driverless vehicles, Pony.ai will still have safety supervisors in the front passenger seats initially.

title: China grants driverless taxi permits to Baidu, Pony.ai
summary: China has granted Baidu and Pony.ai permits to operate driverless ride-hailing services to the public in Beijing, marking a significant step in the nation's driverless technology ambitions. These permits allow the companies to offer rides without a safety driver behind the wheel, though a safety supervisor is still required in the front passenger seat. Baidu has already started offering these services in a 60km2 area in suburban Beijing, continuing its heavy investment in autonomous driving and AI technology.

title: Trip.com reveals latest ownership: Chairman Liang, major investor Baidu added millions of shares
summary: Trip.com Group's co-founder and Chairman James Liang increased his stake in the company from 3.1% to 3.6% by adding over 21.6 million shares. Search engine giant Baidu, the largest stakeholder, also added over 60 million shares, maintaining its 10.8% stake. In 2021, the company reported an annual revenue of RMB 20,029 million (USD 3,031 million), with 8% generated from international markets.

title: Family offices face scrutiny as CFTC boss vows no Archegos redux
summary: The top U.S. derivatives regulator, Rostin Behnam, is increasing scrutiny on family offices following the Archegos Capital Management collapse. Behnam stated that the Archegos situation highlighted the need to re-evaluate agency rules and the dangers of individual investors building massive, unregulated swaps positions. The CFTC may propose new rules for investors with significant swaps positions and is reviewing risk management practices among private funds, prime brokers, and banks.

title: Baidu Releases 2021 ESG Report, Pledging Efforts towards a Greener Future with Technology Innovation
summary: Baidu released its 2021 ESG report, detailing its efforts to promote a sustainable economy through technological innovation. The report highlights Baidu's commitment to achieving carbon neutrality by 2030, improving corporate governance with a "three lines" risk management model, and fostering AI development through open-source projects. Additionally, Baidu emphasized its dedication to diversity and inclusiveness, with women representing 43.2% of its management team in 2021.

title: Baidu’s Chip Affiliate Kunlun Eyes Fundraise at $2.5 Billion Valuation
summary: Baidu's chip affiliate, Kunlun, is seeking to raise 2 billion yuan ($317 million) in a new funding round, which would value the company at $2.5 billion. This initiative reflects a trend in China's venture capital landscape, where investors are pivoting from internet businesses towards core technologies like semiconductors, aligning with Beijing's strategic priorities. If successful, this would be Kunlun's second funding round, increasing its valuation significantly from its previous round.

title: Baidu expands its self-driving Apollo Go fleet in Beijing by 20 cars
summary: Baidu has expanded its self-driving Apollo Go fleet in Beijing by adding 20 unmanned taxi licenses, increasing its total number of autonomous vehicles. The company is now operating the second-largest unmanned autonomous driving fleet in China, with 300 unmanned pickup points in Yixhuang. Baidu's Apollo Moon program uses various vehicle types and has achieved improvements in self-driving technology and passenger features.

title: Baidu beats revenue estimates helped by AI, cloud services
summary: Baidu, China's search engine giant, exceeded quarterly revenue predictions due to increased demand for its cloud and AI products, spurred by COVID-19 restrictions. Despite this, the company reported a net loss and cautioned that the second quarter would be more challenging due to ongoing pandemic impacts. Revenue for Baidu Core, excluding online ad sales, grew, with AI cloud sales jumping significantly.

title: Baidu Inc continues to grow and invest in R&D
summary: Baidu Inc reported a 1 percent year-on-year revenue increase to 28.4 billion yuan in Q1, with a non-GAAP net profit of 3.9 billion yuan, and continues heavy investment in R&D, which rose 10 percent to 5.6 billion yuan. The company's cloud and intelligent driving businesses, particularly Apollo Go, show strong growth despite COVID-19 related impacts. Baidu has also secured permits for driverless robotaxis in Beijing and Chongqing, highlighting advancements in its AI initiatives.

title: How China’s canned wine brand WiMo took over drinks market in a year
summary: WiMo, a Chinese canned wine brand established in 2021, has quickly become a rising star in China's rapidly growing low-alcohol drinks market. The brand successfully raised millions in funding, signaling strong investor confidence in the sector. WiMo targets younger consumers with approachable, often sparkling and fruit-flavored wines in casual can packaging, distinguishing itself from traditional wine perceptions.

title: Baidu's electric vehicle firm Jidu unveils first 'robot' car
summary: Baidu's EV arm Jidu Auto has unveiled its first "robot" concept car, aiming for mass production in 2023. This vehicle, co-funded by Chinese automaker Geely, features Level 4 autonomous capabilities and voice recognition, and is designed to attract tech-savvy users. The move positions Baidu among global tech companies developing smart cars following Tesla's success.

title: Google Engineer Claims AI System is Sentient; China's Tech Giants Flourish as Crackdown Eases
summary: A Google engineer has been suspended after claiming that the company's LaMDA chatbot system shows signs of sentience, which Google denies, citing confidentiality breaches. Meanwhile, China's major tech companies like Baidu, Tencent, and Alibaba are experiencing a surge in share value as Beijing eases its regulatory crackdown on the sector. Twitter has also introduced a new "people-first" content moderation process to improve reporting of offensive content, especially amid Elon Musk's previous concerns about fake accounts.

title: Jidu Auto steps up to roll out mass-produced robocars
summary: Jidu Auto, a joint venture between Baidu and Geely, plans to launch its first mass-produced robocar model in the fall and open its first self-owned store in Q4 this year. The mass-produced version will feature Level 4 autonomous driving capability and targets the family car market, priced above 200,000 yuan. Leveraging Baidu's AI and autonomous driving software and Geely's manufacturing expertise, Jidu aims to deliver fully driverless transportation experiences, with orders starting in the second half of the year and deliveries commencing in 2023.

title: China's Baidu races Waymo, GM to develop self-driving cars
summary: Baidu is a key contender in the global race to develop self-driving cars, competing with companies like Alphabet Inc.'s Waymo and General Motors Co.'s Cruise. Baidu and Pony.ai recently secured China's first licenses for driverless taxi operations with a safety supervisor, trailing Waymo's 2020 launch in Phoenix. The company's Apollo Go service has become a leader in ride volume, and Baidu is investing billions in R&D to make autonomous driving cheaper, safer, and more efficient, expanding to 65 cities by 2025.

title: Gurus foresee 'virtual humans' enhancing many industries
summary: Experts predict that virtual humans, fueled by AI advancements, will revolutionize various industries such as retail, finance, and education. Production costs and development times for digital avatars are expected to decrease significantly, leading to widespread adoption. Companies like Baidu are already investing heavily in this technology, with the market in China projected to reach 10.24 billion yuan ($1.5 billion) by 2026.

title: What is Baidu? The Chinese Search Engine Giant
summary: Baidu is China's leading search engine, founded in 2000 by Robin Li and Eric Xu, and is one of the world's largest technology companies. Beyond its dominant search engine, Baidu offers services like maps, translation, e-commerce, and has invested heavily in artificial intelligence and autonomous driving. The publicly traded company, listed on NASDAQ as "BIDU," faces scrutiny for its adherence to Chinese government censorship policies, which affect its search results and content promotion.

title: Baidu Unveils Next-Gen Autonomous Vehicle, Ready to Provide Driverless Robotaxi Half of Taxi Fares
summary: Baidu has unveiled its next-generation fully autonomous vehicle, Apollo RT6, an all-electric robotaxi with a detachable steering wheel designed for complex urban environments. Set to launch on Baidu's Apollo Go ride-hailing service in China in 2023, the Apollo RT6 boasts a significantly reduced production cost of RMB 250,000 per unit. This cost reduction is expected to enable the deployment of tens of thousands of AVs, aiming to make robotaxi services half the price of traditional taxis.

title: Baidu Races Ahead of Tesla With Launch of Robotaxi With Detachable Steering Wheel
summary: Baidu has launched a new autonomous car with a detachable steering wheel, priced at about $37,000, which it intends to use for its robotaxi service by 2023. This move positions Baidu to deploy such vehicles a year ahead of Tesla's plans for mass production of a similar car. The Chinese search giant aims to significantly reduce the cost of autonomous vehicles and expand its robotaxi fleet.

title: Baidu unveils autonomous vehicle without steering wheel
summary: Baidu Inc. has unveiled a new autonomous vehicle (AV) with a detachable steering wheel, planning to deploy it for its robotaxi service in China next year. The new model will significantly reduce costs to 250,000 yuan per unit, enabling a larger deployment across China and aiming to make robotaxi rides half the cost of traditional taxis. This Level 4 autonomous vehicle features advanced sensors, and Baidu is awaiting regulatory approval to operate it without a steering wheel.

title: Baidu Granted China's First-Ever Permits for Commercial Fully Driverless Ride-Hailing Services
summary: Baidu has received China's first-ever permits to operate commercial fully driverless robotaxi services on open roads in Chongqing and Wuhan, allowing the company to collect fares without human safety operators in the vehicles. This milestone marks a significant step for the future of mobility in China, making it the first country globally to allow fared fully driverless robotaxi operation in two megacities. Baidu's Apollo Go, already the world's largest robotaxi service provider, will offer these services during designated daytime hours.

title: Baidu bags China's first fully driverless robotaxi licenses
summary: Baidu has received the first permits in China to operate fully driverless robotaxi services on open roads in Chongqing and Wuhan. These permits allow commercial robotaxis to operate without human safety drivers and mark a significant turning point for autonomous driving policy in China. Baidu plans to deploy a batch of five fee-charging robotaxis in designated areas within each city.

title: China's autonomous driving enters 'fast lane' with commercial operations
summary: China is making significant strides in autonomous driving, with commercial robotaxi services recently launched in Wuhan and Chongqing. Companies like Baidu, WeRide, and DeepRoute.ai are at the forefront, supported by favorable government policies and the complex urban traffic environments that help refine the technology. The industry aims to expand operations, enhance safety, and build an industrial chain for mass production while increasing public acceptance.

title: China's Baidu reveals its first quantum computer called Qianshi
summary: Baidu, the Chinese search engine giant, unveiled its first quantum computer, "Qianshi," featuring a 10-qubit processor, making it available to external users. The company also developed a 36-qubit quantum chip as it joins the global race in quantum computing. This technology, seen as a cornerstone for future global supremacy, is attracting significant investment from governments and major tech companies like IBM and Google.

title: Baidu Releases Superconducting Quantum Computer and World's First All-Platform Integration Solution, Making Quantum Computing Within Reach
summary: Baidu has launched "Qian Shi," its first industry-level superconducting quantum computer, and "Liang Xi," the world's first all-platform quantum hardware-software integration solution. These innovations aim to make quantum computing more accessible for industrial application by allowing users to access quantum computational resources via mobile app, PC, and cloud, integrating hardware, software, and applications. The new offerings are expected to advance the industrialization of quantum computing and simplify its use for practical applications like material design and protein folding.

title: Baidu Capital Appoints Li Xiaoyang as New CEO
summary: Baidu Capital has announced the appointment of Li Xiaoyang as its new CEO. This move signifies a leadership change at the investment arm of the Chinese technology giant, Baidu. Details about his prior roles or strategic direction were not provided in this brief announcement.

title: Baidu Stock Is Tumbling. An Earnings Beat Can’t Offset Falling Revenue.
summary: Baidu's stock is falling despite an earnings beat, as declining revenue highlights the impact of China's economic slowdown on its tech giants. The company's financial performance reflects broader challenges within the world's second-largest economy.

title: Baidu Swings Back to Profit Even as Revenue Slips
summary: Baidu Inc. returned to profitability in the second quarter of 2022, reporting a net profit despite a 5% decline in total revenue. The company's online advertising revenue dropped due to Covid-19 disruptions, but its cloud business saw significant growth. Baidu is focusing on autonomous driving, AI, and cloud computing for future growth, with its AI Cloud holding an 8.4% market share in China.

title: Baidu Announces Superconducting Quantum Computer and Integration Solution
summary: Baidu has unveiled its first quantum computing processor, Qian Shi, a 10-qubit superconducting quantum device, and a comprehensive "all-platform" integration solution called Liang Xi. Liang Xi aims to simplify access to quantum hardware resources via mobile, desktop, and the Cloud, making quantum computing more accessible for researchers and companies. Baidu's quantum software stack includes Quanlse for quantum control, Paddle Quantum for quantum machine learning, and Quantum Leaf for programming and simulating quantum computers.

title: Digital fashion avatars bolster livestreaming
summary: Digital fashion avatars and virtual fashion shows are increasingly being used in China's fashion and livestreaming industries, offering immersive experiences and reducing operational costs. Tech companies like Baidu are leveraging AI to advance virtual human technology, which is expected to drive the market to 270 billion yuan by 2030. While offering significant advantages in design and marketing, experts also highlight challenges such as data security and the need for more natural human-digital interactions.

title: China's car sales rose 21.2% in September: auto industry body CPCA
summary: China's passenger car sales increased by 21.2% year-on-year in September, reaching 1.95 million units, according to the China Passenger Car Association (CPCA). Tesla, an American electric vehicle manufacturer, sold 83,135 China-made cars during the same month, including 5,522 for export.

title: Without Safeguards, Quantum Computing Risks Exacerbating Global Inequalities
summary: The article discusses how the rapid development of quantum computing, dominated by a few developed nations and companies, risks widening global inequalities. It highlights the potential for quantum technology to revolutionize various sectors while also posing significant threats, especially concerning internet security and national security if not governed by ethical and legal safeguards. The author emphasizes the need for a more inclusive approach to quantum research and development to prevent a scenario where developing nations are left vulnerable and dependent on quantum-dominant countries.

title: Tim Draper turns attention from China to Taiwan
summary: US venture capitalist Tim Draper, an early investor in Tesla and SpaceX, has shifted his investment focus from China to Taiwan. He stated that he stopped investing in China due to early signs that it was moving away from a free market under President Xi Jinping, and he now sees Taiwan as a natural place for investment, particularly in its booming tech and semiconductor sector. Draper also expressed concerns about a potential Chinese attack on Taiwan and praised Elon Musk's ability to get things done.

title: China’s Baidu Sees Little Impact From U.S. Chip Controls
summary: Baidu, the Chinese search-engine and AI giant, believes new U.S. export restrictions on advanced semiconductors will have minimal short-term impact. An executive stated the company has stockpiled enough high-end chips for its needs. Baidu also anticipates that its AI businesses may even benefit from the new rules in the long run.

title: China's Baidu beats Q3 revenue estimates as ad sales recover
summary: Baidu Inc. exceeded third-quarter revenue expectations thanks to a recovery in online advertising sales and growth in its cloud and artificial intelligence (AI) businesses. The company's revenue rose 2% year-on-year to 32.54 billion yuan, surpassing analyst estimates. Baidu also reported significant growth in non-online marketing revenue, particularly from its cloud and AI segments, and continued to expand its self-driving technology with its Apollo Go robotaxi service.

title: Baidu's Kunlun Xin mass produces 7nm XPU-R chips for autonomous driving
summary: Baidu's semiconductor unit, Kunlun Xin, is mass-producing 2G chips based on its XPU-R architecture, which are 7nm and feature high-speed GDDR6 DRAM, making them suitable for various processors and servers in China. These chips are designed to enhance computing power and compatibility for advanced AI applications, particularly in autonomous driving. While current chips are 2G, Kunlun Xin is actively developing 3G and 4G versions for future integration into Baidu's Apollo Go robotaxi services.

title: Chinese tech giant Baidu to build world’s largest fully driverless ride-hailing service area in 2023
summary: Baidu announced plans to create the world's largest fully driverless ride-hailing service area in 2023, expanding its Apollo Go robotaxi operations. The company also revealed technological advancements in autonomous driving, including an AI model for perception and high-definition maps. This development, alongside governmental policy support and rapid growth in ride numbers, signifies China's accelerated progress in the autonomous driving sector.

title: No, you can’t buy a self-driving car yet, but a lot of people seem to think you can
summary: Despite widespread misconceptions, fully self-driving cars (Level 3-5 autonomy) are not yet available for purchase in Canada or the U.S. Many consumers mistakenly believe that current driver-assist technologies, like Tesla's Autopilot or GM's Super Cruise (which are Level 2), provide full self-driving capabilities, leading to dangerous misuse. Experts emphasize that drivers must remain attentive as these systems are only aids and not replacements for human operators, and more advanced systems are still in limited testing or decades away.

title: AI-generated content helping redefine industry
summary: Chinese tech companies are significantly investing in the artificial intelligence-generated content (AIGC) sector, driven by the success of models like ChatGPT. Experts believe AIGC will revolutionize content creation by assisting humans and eventually generating content independently, with diverse applications in industries from finance to education. Companies like Baidu, ByteDance, and Kuaishou are already deploying AI-powered tools, signaling a major shift in content production and innovation.

title: Baidu to Add 200 Fully Driverless Robotaxis to Fleet in 2023, Aiming for World's Largest Fully Driverless Ride-hailing Area
summary: Baidu announced plans to add an additional 200 fully driverless robotaxis to its fleet in 2023, with the goal of creating the world's largest fully driverless ride-hailing area. This expansion follows Baidu receiving Beijing's first license to test fully driverless vehicles without a safety operator, building on its successful operations in Wuhan and Chongqing. The company's Apollo Go service is rapidly expanding its coverage and operating hours, leveraging a decade of R&D and significant test mileage in autonomous driving technology.

title: Baidu, Pony.ai start driverless robotaxi tests in Beijing
summary: Baidu and Pony.ai have secured the first licenses to test fully autonomous vehicles without safety operators in Beijing. Both companies will deploy 10 driverless vehicles each in a Beijing technology park, moving closer to commercial robotaxi services. Baidu, focusing on self-driving for diversification, aims for robotaxi rides to be half the cost of traditional commercial car rides and plans to expand its robotaxi network.

title: Baidu Research Unveils Top 10 Tech Trends for 2023
summary: Baidu Research has released its predictions for the top 10 technology trends of 2023, encompassing areas like big models, digital-real convergence, autonomous driving, and sustainability. These trends highlight the increasing integration of AI into various sectors and its role in addressing global challenges. Baidu will showcase innovations related to these trends at its annual AI developer conference, Baidu Create, on January 10, 2023.

title: Baidu Create 2022 Outlines New Strategy for AI Development Based on Feedback-Driven Innovation
summary: Baidu's annual developer conference, Baidu Create 2022, outlined a new strategy for AI development focused on feedback-driven innovation, emphasizing deep learning as the cornerstone for technological revolution. The company introduced key advancements in AIGC technologies, intelligent transportation with UniBEV, and efficiency in metaverse space construction using XiRang MetaStack. Baidu also detailed its QIAN strategy for quantum computing and new computing architectures, alongside a commitment to train millions of AI talents to foster a stronger AI ecosystem.

title: Urbanites’ mental health undermined by air pollution
summary: A large-scale study in China reveals that air pollution significantly undermines the mental health of urban populations, with effects becoming more pronounced with increased exposure duration. Men, middle-aged individuals, and married people are identified as more vulnerable. The negative impact of air pollution on mental health is found to be reduced in cities with higher GDP per capita, greater health resources, larger green spaces, and more sports facilities, suggesting that improvements in urban living conditions and social welfare can mitigate these effects.

title: Baidu Plans Rival to ChatGPT. China’s Google Wants to Be Its AI Champion.
summary: Baidu, often referred to as China's Google, is reportedly developing its own AI-powered chatbot to rival ChatGPT. This move aligns with CEO Robin Li's strategy to shift the company's focus towards artificial intelligence and cloud computing. The initiative highlights Baidu's ambition to become a leader in the AI space within China.

title: China's Baidu to launch ChatGPT-style bot in March - source
summary: Chinese internet search giant Baidu Inc is reportedly planning to launch its own artificial intelligence chatbot service, similar to OpenAI's ChatGPT, in March. The service is expected to be a standalone application initially and then integrated into Baidu's search engine, aiming to provide more professional, human-like responses to user queries beyond just links. Baidu has been heavily investing in AI technology to diversify its revenue streams.

title: Baidu Stock Jumps. It's Launching an AI Chatbot Called 'ERNIE Bot.'
summary: Chinese tech giant Baidu announced its plans to launch an AI chatbot named "ERNIE Bot," designed to compete with popular platforms like ChatGPT. This news has significantly boosted Baidu's stock, as investors are keen on artificial intelligence opportunities. The company is rapidly expanding its presence in the AI sector.

title: Baidu Stock Surges on Plan for ChatGPT-Style Bot
summary: Baidu's stock surged after the Chinese search-engine giant announced plans to launch its own ChatGPT-style AI chatbot, named "Ernie bot," next month. The move positions Baidu to compete in the global AI commercialization race alongside other tech giants. Baidu's American depositary receipts and Hong Kong-listed stock saw significant gains following the announcement.

title: iQIYI Reaches Framework Agreement with Baidu on AI-Generated Content
summary: iQIYI, a leading Chinese online entertainment video service provider, has announced a framework agreement with Baidu to integrate Baidu's ERNIE Bot, a generative dialogue AI product. This partnership aims to leverage AI-Generated Content (AIGC) technologies across iQIYI's business, including content search, promotion, and novel creation, to enhance creative capabilities and user experiences. iQIYI will receive priority access to ERNIE Bot to foster innovation in the film and television industry.

title: Samsung ramps up smartphone efforts in China
summary: Samsung Electronics Co Ltd is intensifying its localization efforts in China with the launch of its new Galaxy S23 series, aiming to capture a larger share of the premium smartphone market. The company has forged partnerships with Shanghai Disney Resort, Mango TV, and Baidu Inc to promote its new handsets, which feature upgraded AI-powered cameras. This strategic push comes as China's smartphone market experienced a 14 percent year-on-year decline in shipments last year, marking the first time since 2013 that shipments fell below 300 million units.

title: Baidu's ChatGPT-like app will revolutionise its search engine, says CEO
summary: Baidu's CEO Robin Li announced that its upcoming ChatGPT-like app, Ernie Bot, will revolutionize its search engine by enhancing user experience and engagement, significantly expanding the market size of search engines. The company plans to build an AI ecosystem around Ernie Bot, leveraging its advanced generative AI technology to boost online advertising and serve as a productivity tool for other businesses. Baidu aims to regain market share and capitalize on its "first mover" advantage in China's AI market with Ernie Bot, which is trained on years of search data and is "state of the art" in understanding Chinese language and culture.

title: Demo of China’s Answer to ChatGPT Disappoints Investors
summary: Baidu Inc.'s CEO Robin Li unveiled "Ernie Bot," China's answer to ChatGPT, in a recorded video demonstration that disappointed investors, leading to a 6.4% drop in Baidu's shares. The pre-recorded nature of the demo and lack of real-time interaction raised questions about Ernie's capabilities compared to OpenAI's ChatGPT. Despite the initial disappointment, Baidu is considered a frontrunner in China's AI race, planning to integrate Ernie Bot across its services while navigating concerns about US tech restrictions and Chinese regulatory oversight.

title: Baidu Unveils ERNIE Bot, the Latest Generative AI Mastering Chinese Language and Multi-Modal Generation
summary: Baidu has introduced ERNIE Bot, a new-generation large language model (LLM) and generative AI product. It excels in Chinese language understanding, multi-modal generation, literary creation, business writing, and mathematical calculations. The bot will initially be accessible to invited users and enterprise clients via Baidu AI Cloud.

title: Baidu Wins China Capital City's First-Ever Permit to Provide Fully Driverless Ride-hailing Service
summary: Baidu has received the first-ever permits to provide fully driverless ride-hailing services in Beijing, making it a global pioneer as the first provider of such services in a capital city without human drivers or safety operators. This achievement expands Baidu's fully driverless operations to three major Chinese cities: Beijing, Wuhan, and Chongqing. The company plans to deploy 10 fully driverless vehicles in Beijing and aims to build the world's largest fully driverless ride-hailing area in 2023, leveraging its extensive autonomous driving technology and patent portfolio.

title: Baidu Inc. (BIDU) Stock Sinks As Market Gains: What You Should Know
summary: Baidu Inc. (BIDU) shares fell by 1.89% while the broader market gained, showing underperformance compared to the S&P 500 and Nasdaq. Despite this, analysts project strong earnings growth for Baidu in its upcoming report and for the full year, with the company currently holding a Zacks Rank of #1 (Strong Buy). Its valuation also appears favorable with a Forward P/E of 13.34 compared to its industry average.

title: Apple sued to stop App Store clones of Baidu's Ernie AI chatbot
summary: Baidu has initiated legal action against Apple and various app developers in Beijing, seeking the removal of numerous unauthorized "Ernie bot" applications from the App Store. The Chinese tech giant claims that these apps are fake, as Baidu has not yet released an official application for its Ernie AI chatbot, which is similar to ChatGPT. Baidu has warned users that any Ernie app currently available is counterfeit and has urged caution against selling access codes for the bot.

title: Baidu sues Apple, app developers over fake Ernie bot apps
summary: Baidu has filed lawsuits against app developers and Apple Inc. over counterfeit versions of its AI-powered Ernie bot app appearing on Apple's App Store. The Chinese search engine giant clarified that no official Ernie app currently exists and warned against purchasing access codes. Baidu's Ernie bot, launched last month, is considered China's answer to ChatGPT.

title: Baidu Creates $140 Million Fund to Back ChatGPT-Like Startups
summary: Baidu Inc. has launched a 1 billion yuan ($140 million) fund to support Chinese startups developing generative AI, similar to ChatGPT services. This initiative aims to incubate projects built on Baidu's Ernie AI model, with individual deployments potentially reaching 10 million yuan. Venture capitalists like IDG Capital will assist in evaluating pitches and providing seed funding to promising founders.

title: China supports commercialization of Level-3 autonomous driving technology
summary: China's Ministry of Industry and Information Technology (MIIT) is actively supporting the commercialization of Level-3 and higher autonomous driving technologies, aiming to accelerate innovation through collaboration and infrastructure development. This governmental backing is pushing China to the forefront of autonomous driving development, with a projected market value of $98.89 billion by 2030. Companies like Baidu, which already operates the world's largest autonomous ride-hailing service, are key players in this expansion.

title: Institutions own 40% of Baidu, Inc. (NASDAQ:BIDU) shares but individual investors control 40% of the company
summary: Individual investors and institutional investors each own 40% of Baidu, Inc. (NASDAQ:BIDU) shares, indicating significant influence from the general public. The CEO, Yanhong Li, holds a 20% stake, while the top 25 shareholders collectively own less than 50% of the company, preventing any single individual from having majority control. This ownership structure suggests that despite institutional presence, individual investors can collectively impact company policies.

title: What Happens When You Ask a Chinese Chatbot About Taiwan? (Published 2023)
summary: This article compares the responses of China's Baidu chatbot, Ernie 3.5, and OpenAI's ChatGPT (GPT-4) to a series of questions, particularly those related to sensitive political topics in China like Taiwan, "zero Covid," and the Tiananmen Square protests. While ChatGPT answered controversial questions straightforwardly, Ernie tended to avoid or reshape answers according to the official Chinese stance, sometimes even rebooting when pressed on highly censored subjects. The comparison highlights the differing approaches and limitations of AI models operating within distinct geopolitical and censorship environments.

title: Baidu Showcases Major AI Developments at WAVE SUMMIT 2023: ERNIE Bot Plugins, PaddlePaddle V2.5, and AI Coding Assistant
summary: Baidu announced significant AI developments at WAVE SUMMIT 2023, including an expansion of ERNIE Bot with five new plugins for enhanced functionalities like data analysis and text-to-video conversion. The company also unveiled the upgraded PaddlePaddle deep learning platform (V2.5), the AI-native application InfoFlow, and the Baidu Comate AI coding assistant for enterprise developers. These updates aim to advance artificial general intelligence and boost developer efficiency.

title: Baidu Revenue Beats as ChatGPT-Style AI Awaits Beijing Approval
summary: Baidu Inc. reported a significant revenue increase, its largest in over a year, with sales jumping 15% to 34.1 billion yuan and net income rising 43% to 5.2 billion yuan for the June quarter. This growth follows a relaxation of Beijing's regulations on the private sector and a rebound in advertising. The company's shares rose in pre-market trading, as investors keenly monitor its ChatGPT-style AI, Ernie Bot, which is awaiting government approval.

title: Baidu Inc (NASDAQ: BIDU): Will BIDU Stock Break Out of $150 Mark?
summary: Baidu Inc (NASDAQ: BIDU) stock has been consolidating within a horizontal range of $123.07 and $151.40, with current trading at $142.60. Despite gaining nearly 20% year-to-date and trading above key moving averages, the stock needs increased buying volume to break out above the $150 mark. Technical indicators show mixed signals, with the RSI indicating a downside trend but MACD confirming upward momentum, necessitating investor patience until the stock sustains above the consolidation phase.

title: Chinese tech giant Baidu gets into EV game with Geely
summary: Baidu has partnered with Zhejiang Geely to launch the electric vehicle brand Ji Yue, with its first model, the 01 crossover, revealed in August. Ji Yue vehicles will integrate Baidu's AI and self-driving technologies with Geely's manufacturing expertise, differentiating themselves from U.S. tech companies that have stalled in their automotive ventures. This move signifies Baidu's deeper expansion into the automotive sector beyond its existing robotaxi services.

title: Bernstein Maintains Baidu Inc - ADR (BIDU) Outperform Recommendation
summary: Bernstein has reiterated its Outperform recommendation for Baidu Inc - ADR (BIDU). Analysts project a 38.47% upside for BIDU, with an average one-year price target of $183.27. Institutional ownership remains significant despite a slight decrease in the number of funds holding the stock.

title: Baidu Launches ERNIE 4.0 Foundation Model, Leading a New Wave of AI-Native Applications
summary: Baidu launched its ERNIE 4.0 foundation model, highlighting its dramatically improved performance in understanding, generation, reasoning, and memory. The company showcased how ERNIE 4.0 powers a new wave of AI-native applications, including enhanced versions of Baidu Search, Baidu GBI, Infoflow, Baidu Wenku, Baidu Drive, and Baidu Maps. This advancement aims to drive new innovations and a thriving AI ecosystem, with a focus on enterprise solutions and expanding autonomous driving capabilities.

title: Baidu's AI Chatbot Signifies China Closing Gap with US
summary: Baidu has launched ERNIE 4.0, an AI chatbot it claims rivals OpenAI's GPT-4, signaling China's rapid advancements in generative AI. This development highlights China's significant investments in AI research and talent, challenging the U.S.'s previous dominance in the field. Despite U.S. efforts to restrict chip exports, China's commitment to AI innovation remains strong, with Baidu integrating AI into many of its applications and other Chinese tech giants also unveiling their own models.

title: Tesla (TSLA) Faces Self-Driving Challenge as Geely and Baidu Launch EV Jiyue 01
summary: Geely and Baidu have launched their first electric vehicle, the Jiyue 01, intensifying competition in China's automated-driving market. This new SUV utilizes Baidu's Apollo platform and aims to transition to a pure-vision driving approach, similar to Tesla, eschewing more expensive sensors. Its introduction presents a direct challenge to Tesla's presence in the self-driving EV sector.

title: Geely Holding and Baidu launch new AI-powered EV
summary: Geely Holding Group and Baidu Inc's joint venture has launched its first AI-powered electric vehicle, the Ji Yue 01, priced from 249,900 yuan ($34,151.8). This SUV features Level 4 autonomous driving technology, a voice-activated control system, advanced intelligent cabins, and integrates Baidu's Ernie Bot for an AI interactive experience. The vehicle boasts a range of up to 720 kilometers on a single charge and marks a significant milestone in the partnership formed in March 2021.

title: Jidu launches AI-based robocar in China
summary: Jidu Auto, a joint venture between Geely and Baidu, has launched its first AI-powered battery electric vehicle, the Ji Yue 01, in China. This "robocar" features Baidu's Apollo Level 4 autonomous driving technology and advanced intelligent cockpit systems. Despite cutting-edge technology, the CEO acknowledges the need to build brand awareness to gain significant market share.

title: Baidu CEO warns China's rush to develop AI models risks wasting resources
summary: Baidu CEO Robin Li warned that China's rapid development of large language AI models risks wasting resources, urging companies to focus instead on practical applications. He noted that while many foundational models have been created, there's a significant lack of corresponding AI applications. Li highlighted that 238 large language models were released by October, up from 79 in June, indicating a substantial investment in basic infrastructure that may not yet have clear business models.

title: Baidu Bruised By Markets Skittish About Misleading Newspaper Report
summary: Baidu's stock took a hit after a misleading report from the South China Morning Post suggested the company collaborated with the Chinese military, leading to a $4.68 billion market cap loss. The report, which the newspaper later corrected, wrongly claimed a "physical link" between a military AI system and Baidu's Ernie Bot, when researchers had only used the public model like any other user. This incident highlights the challenges tech companies face with dual-use technologies and national military ties, especially for Chinese firms under scrutiny for potential US sanctions.

title: Baidu Responds to Recent Media Reports on Ernie Bot
summary: Baidu has issued a clarification regarding recent media reports about an academic paper that mentioned its ERNIE Bot alongside other large language models. The company stated that ERNIE Bot is publicly available and was used by the paper's authors like any other generative AI tool, denying any special business collaboration or tailored services provided to them. Baidu also noted that the South China Morning Post, which first reported on the paper, has since clarified its original report.

title: US “will probably sanction” Baidu despite Chinese military AI denials
summary: Baidu is likely to face US sanctions due to reports linking its AI chatbot 'Ernie' to China's People's Liberation Army (PLA). An academic paper from a PLA-affiliated university claimed the PLA tested its large language model on Baidu's Ernie bot, causing Baidu's shares to slump. The US is expected to respond with sanctions and a ban on Baidu products for politicians and military members, similar to actions taken against Huawei.

title: With 40% ownership of the shares, Baidu, Inc. (NASDAQ:BIDU) is heavily dominated by institutional owners
summary: Baidu, Inc. is significantly influenced by institutional investors who own 40% of the shares, impacting the stock price through their trading actions. Additionally, the CEO, Yanhong Li, holds a substantial 20% ownership, indicating strong insider commitment. The general public also has a notable 40% stake, contributing to the company's ownership diversity.

title: Baidu AI Cloud Collaborates with Samsung China: Galaxy AI Integrates ERNIE LLM
summary: Baidu AI Cloud has announced a strategic partnership with Samsung China, integrating Baidu's ERNIE large language model into Samsung's Galaxy AI. This collaboration aims to provide Chinese users with advanced AI features, including translation and content summarization in call and note applications, leveraging Baidu's LLM technology for enhanced productivity and communication. This move signifies the growing trend of integrating generative AI into mobile devices.

title: Baidu feels benefit from AI, Q4 revenue rises 6%
summary: Baidu reported a 6% rise in fourth-quarter revenue, reaching 34.95 billion yuan ($4.92 billion), primarily driven by its generative AI applications. The company is actively monetizing its AI services, including a premium version of Ernie Bot and partnerships to integrate its technology into smartphones. Baidu's adjusted net income saw a significant 44% increase to 7.76 billion yuan, surpassing analyst expectations.

title: After Earnings, Is Baidu Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Baidu released its Q4 earnings, revealing revenue in line with expectations but lower operating margins due to AI project costs and declining advertising revenue. Morningstar maintains a 5-star rating and a $183 fair value estimate, despite near-term macroeconomic and geopolitical risks, seeing long-term potential in Baidu's AI products and cloud services. The company's wide economic moat is attributed to its dominant user base and extensive R&D in AI, though competition and uncertainty around AI monetization remain concerns.

title: Apple’s stock has faltered, but a Steve Jobs moment in AI may be on the horizon
summary: Apple's stock performance has been underwhelming, leading to concerns about its AI capabilities. However, an analyst suggests that Apple CEO Tim Cook might be preparing a "Steve Jobs moment" by announcing an AI app store, similar to how Wall Street initially underestimated the iTunes Store. This could represent a significant play in the artificial intelligence sector, despite previous doubts fueled by reports of Apple discussing AI engines with other tech giants.

title: Baidu Shares Rise After Reports That Apple Will Use Its AI Services in China Products
summary: Baidu's shares increased significantly in Hong Kong following reports that Apple plans to utilize Baidu's AI services for its products in China. The collaboration, which has been the subject of preliminary talks, would make Baidu Apple's local generative AI model provider for the iPhone 16, Mac operating system, and iOS 18. This partnership is pending approval from Chinese regulators for generative AI models.

title: Baidu Create 2024: Empowering Everyone as Creators of the AI Future
summary: Baidu is hosting its annual AI developer conference, Baidu Create 2024, on April 16th in Shenzhen, themed "Create the Future." The event will showcase new ERNIE models and development toolkits, aiming to make AI-native application development more accessible to everyone. Baidu's CEO, Robin Li, will deliver a keynote addressing the integration of general AI capabilities with industry expertise and introducing lightweight models and AI toolkits for non-programmers.

title: Update: Baidu faces intense PR backlash following controversial comments by its PR chief on social media
summary: Baidu's vice president and head of PR, Qu Jing, has reportedly left the company following intense public backlash over controversial comments she made on social media. Her remarks, which criticized employees and showed a lack of empathy, sparked outrage and led to a public apology from Qu. The incident negatively impacted Baidu's stock price and raised questions about corporate culture and employee treatment in large tech companies.

title: After Earnings, Is Baidu Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Baidu's Q1 earnings report shows lingering advertising headwinds, but potential long-term growth driven by generative AI. Morningstar maintains a 4-star "undervalued" rating with a fair value estimate of $174 per share, anticipating AI Cloud and smart driving to be key growth drivers offsetting near-term advertising weakness. The company holds a wide economic moat and strong financial health despite competitive and uncertain AI market risks.

title: Baidu Introduces AI and Agent-Driven Innovations at Annual Mobile Ecosystem Event to Transform Mobile Experience
summary: Baidu unveiled its latest AI and agent-driven innovations at its annual Mobile Ecosystem Group event, integrating large language models and ERNIE agents across its mobile products like Baidu Search, Baidu Wenku, and the Baidu App. These advancements aim to make AI more accessible to individuals and enterprises, enhancing productivity and enabling intelligent transformation. Noteworthy achievements include Baidu Wenku's 100 multimodal AI features used over 1.5 billion times and Baidu AgentBuilder supporting over 160,000 developers and users and 50,000 enterprises across various application scenarios.

title: Tesla upgrades in-car navigation software in China, introduces lane-level guidance
summary: Tesla has released a software upgrade for its in-car navigation system in China, introducing new features like lane-level guidance to reduce reliance on smartphone apps. The upgrade, using Baidu Maps V20, aims to provide more detailed road information and improve driving safety. This enhancement comes as Tesla works to introduce its "Full Self-Driving" software in China, while a more detailed navigation has been a common request from Chinese users.

title: How Safe Are Driverless Cars in China? I Rode in Some to See. (Published 2024)
summary: This article explores the safety of driverless cars in China, highlighting a recent fatal crash of an Aito M7 Plus electric SUV where information was censored. The author, Keith Bradsher, describes the official support and public acceptance of computer-aided driving in China, contrasting it with the lack of transparency around safety incidents. It also draws parallels with safety investigations faced by driverless car companies in the United States.

title: Baidu Gets a Boost on Suggestions Its Robotaxi Business Is Taking Off
summary: Baidu's American Depositary Receipts (ADRs) rose on reports that its Apollo Go robotaxi business is expected to break even in Wuhan this year, according to analyst Li Muhua. The robotaxi service is currently conducting manned tests in 11 Chinese cities and unmanned tests in several others, with operating costs anticipated to fall significantly. The positive outlook for Baidu's autonomous driving efforts is also fueled by the upcoming launch of Tesla's robotaxi in August.

title: Baidu gets grant for autonomous excavation learning method using neural network for trajectory planning
summary: Baidu has received a patent for an autonomous excavation learning method that uses a convolutional neural network to predict excavation success probability. This method involves tiling trajectory parameters across a voxel-grid, combining them with a voxel-grid representation of the excavation scene, and feeding this into a neural network to determine the likelihood of a successful excavation. The system also includes a process for refining trajectory parameters through iterations of sampling and selection, ultimately choosing the trajectory with the highest success probability.

title: Meta faces AI accuracy issues as tech industry tackles hallucinations, deepfakes
summary: Meta is addressing accuracy issues with its AI chatbot, Meta AI, after it incorrectly claimed the assassination attempt on Donald Trump didn't happen and misapplied a fact-checking label to a photo. This comes as other tech giants like Google and Baidu also work on improving AI safety and accuracy, with Google focusing on deepfake removal and Baidu developing a "self-reasoning" mechanism for LLMs. The incidents highlight the ongoing challenges of AI hallucinations and the industry's efforts to mitigate them.

title: Baidu stock dropping on flat revenue, China's econ slowdown
summary: Baidu's stock is falling after the Chinese internet giant reported flat second-quarter revenue, impacted by China's economic downturn and reduced advertising spending. The company is facing challenges as it transitions from search ads to AI, despite some traction with its Ernie large language model. Analysts are questioning how long this transition will take for Baidu to regain momentum.

title: Baidu-backed Chinese autonomous trucking firm expects to be profitable in 2025
summary: Baidu-backed Chinese autonomous driving startup CiDi anticipates achieving profitability in 2025 by focusing on software and services for mining and factory applications. The company, valued at $1.2 billion, is open to an IPO and emphasizes industrial-grade haulage where labor cost savings are significant. Despite potential challenges in overseas expansion due to Western concerns over Chinese technologies, CiDi aims for a double-digit share of the global autonomous mining market by 2030.

title: Baidu rebrands Ernie Bot as Wenxiaoyan in China to stand out from AI rivals
summary: Baidu has rebranded its flagship AI app, Ernie Bot, as "Wenxiaoyan" in China to differentiate itself in the competitive AI market. The new name highlights its function as a "new search" assistant, with Baidu focusing on user value and integrating AI capabilities into various products. The app allows for text, speech, and image-based searches, along with AI functions like text-to-image generation and customized agents.

title: Baidu - The Chinese Google at a Discount
summary: The article analyzes Baidu (BIDU), describing it as "The Chinese Google at a Discount." It notes that recent increases in Chinese stock prices, including Baidu's, are driven by depressed valuations due to geopolitical concerns rather than fundamental improvements. Baidu shows strong technical indicators like a 34.07% increase in the last month and a buy signal from Trend Seeker, alongside a P/E of 11.30 and expected revenue growth, indicating it could be an undervalued opportunity despite volatility.

title: Horizon Robotics Starts IPO, with Investment From Alibaba and Baidu
summary: Horizon Robotics, a Chinese AI chip startup, has initiated its initial public offering (IPO) process. The company has attracted investments from major tech giants Alibaba and Baidu, signaling strong market confidence in its growth potential. This move marks a significant step for Horizon Robotics in its journey to expand its presence in the competitive artificial intelligence sector.

title: It's Time To Buy Baidu Again (NASDAQ:BIDU)
summary: Baidu is considered highly undervalued, especially with potential from its AI/robotaxi segment not yet reflected in its stock price, making it a strong buy around $90. Technical analysis suggests the stock has likely bottomed at $80, offering a prime opportunity to invest below $100, with projections of 40-100% gains in the next year despite competitive and geopolitical risks.

title: Baidu Unveils iRAG and No-Code Tool Miaoda to Drive AI Application Boom
summary: Baidu unveiled new AI technologies at its Baidu World 2024 conference, including iRAG for mitigating image generation hallucinations and Miaoda, a no-code tool for building applications using natural language. The company reported that its ERNIE foundation model now handles 1.5 billion daily API calls, a 30-fold increase year-over-year, and emphasized the growing importance of AI agents in application development. Baidu also announced user growth for ERNIE Bot, increased enterprise application development on its Qianfan platform, and the upcoming launch of Xiaodu AI Glasses.

title: Baidu's Apollo autonomous vehicles granted licence to test in Hong Kong
summary: Baidu's Apollo Go robotaxi service has secured a license to test 10 autonomous vehicles in North Lantau, Hong Kong, marking the company's first known permit for self-driving vehicle testing outside mainland China. The trial, valid from December 2024 to December 2029, will initially operate one vehicle on specified roads with a backup operator. This move is part of Baidu's strategy to expand its autonomous driving technology to international markets like Hong Kong, Singapore, and the Middle East.

title: This Chinese AI Stock Is Preparing for ‘Explosive Growth’ in 2025. Is It Too Late to Buy?
summary: Baidu, often referred to as the "Google of China," is rapidly transforming into an AI powerhouse, with its ERNIE Bot and Apollo autonomous driving platform gaining significant traction. Despite a challenging macroeconomic environment impacting its online marketing revenue, Baidu is heavily investing in AI, reporting strong growth in AI Cloud revenue and high user engagement with its generative AI content. Analysts project substantial sales and earnings growth, with the stock currently valued at an attractive multiple, suggesting significant upside potential.

title: Baidu Stock Falls After Earnings. Advertising Slump Eclipses AI Boost.
summary: Baidu's stock fell due to a slowdown in advertising spending, which overshadowed any potential boost from its AI initiatives. The Chinese search-engine provider's quarterly revenue was negatively impacted, leading to a 13% decline in its stock over the past year in Hong Kong.

title: Baidu Stock Slips After Dip In Advertising Revenue Overshadows Q4 Earnings Beat: Retail’s Still Impressed
summary: Baidu's stock (BIDU) declined despite beating Q4 2024 earnings estimates, primarily due to a 7% drop in advertising revenue, which overshadowed its overall financial performance. The company's CEO, Robin Li, highlighted a strategic shift towards an AI-first business, with strong growth in AI Cloud and its Apollo Go robotaxi service. Despite the stock slip, retail sentiment on platforms like Stocktwits remained "extremely bullish," with some investors considering the stock undervalued given its AI advancements.

title: Baidu's Stock Slides as AI Gains Fail to Offset Concerns Over Revenue Drop
summary: Baidu's shares dropped following its latest earnings report, which showed strong growth in its AI and cloud computing businesses but a third consecutive quarterly decline in overall revenue, impacting investor sentiment. While non-online marketing revenue, driven by AI cloud, surged, traditional online marketing income decreased, leading to a slight annual revenue decline. Despite this, Baidu remains optimistic about its AI investments, with analysts forecasting growth in its AI cloud segments and eventual commercialization of AI search.

title: Here's What Analysts Are Forecasting For Baidu, Inc. (NASDAQ:BIDU) After Its Annual Results
summary: Baidu Inc. shares fell 7.2% after its annual results, despite beating EPS expectations. Analysts expect Baidu's revenue to increase by 3.1% in 2025 to CN¥137.3 billion, while EPS is predicted to decline by 11% to CN¥60.62. The consensus among analysts remains largely unchanged, with no major adjustments to revenue or earnings estimates, and the price target holding at US$110.

title: Baidu Stock Lags Despite China’s AI Surge
summary: Despite a surge in China's AI sector driven by models like DeepSeek, Baidu's stock has underperformed its peers like Alibaba and Tencent, rising only about 6% compared to their significant gains. Baidu's struggles are attributed to China's weak post-COVID-19 economic recovery impacting advertising revenue, although its AI cloud division shows strong growth. The company is investing heavily in AI technologies such as Ernie Bot and self-driving, and its stock is considered fairly valued at under 9x consensus 2025 earnings, presenting potential upside as economic stimulus measures take effect.

title: Baidu releases reasoning AI model to take on DeepSeek
summary: Baidu has launched its new Ernie X1 reasoning AI model and upgraded its flagship foundation model to Ernie 4.5, making all tiers of its service free for chatbot users. The company aims to compete with rivals like DeepSeek, whose R1 model demonstrated strong performance at a low cost, and plans to open-source its Ernie AI models from June 30. This strategic shift follows increased competition and reflects Baidu's efforts to reassert its position in the rapidly evolving AI landscape, with an investment of $1.6 billion into AI and cloud infrastructure from a recent acquisition.

title: Baidu Stock Jumps as Chinese Market Leaves U.S. in Its Dust
summary: Baidu's stock surged after the company introduced a new artificial intelligence model, contributing to a broader rally in the Chinese stock market. This contrasts with the performance of U.S. equities, highlighting the momentum building in China. Baidu's AI development is attracting significant investor attention.

title: Baidu shares jump 10% following release of new free-to-use AI models
summary: Baidu's shares increased by 10.7% after the release of two new AI models, including an updated "Ernie" model and a reasoning model competitive with DeepSeek's R1. This move is seen as Baidu's effort to regain leadership in China's AI sector, especially since the new models are free for individual users, a shift from their previous subscription-based approach. Analysts suggest this could help Baidu solidify its technology as an industry standard and expand its market share.

title: Delisting Chinese Stocks Like Alibaba and Baidu Is a Real Possibility for Trump
summary: The article discusses the real possibility of former President Donald Trump delisting major Chinese stocks such as Alibaba and Baidu from American stock exchanges amidst the escalating U.S.-China trade war. This move could have significant implications for these companies, which have already seen their stock prices fall due to the trade tensions. The potential delisting highlights the broader risks and stakes involved for Chinese companies trading on U.S. markets.

title: Baidu number 1 in AI patent applications
summary: Baidu Inc. has cemented its position as a leader in AI patent applications and authorizations in China, topping the domestic list for seven consecutive years. The company has filed over 22,000 AI-related patent applications in China by the end of 2024 and 27,000 globally. Baidu also secured the top global spot for deep learning patent applications and ranked second worldwide (first in China) for large language model-related patents.

title: Baidu Launches ERNIE 4.5 Turbo, ERNIE X1 Turbo and New Suite of AI Tools to Empower Developers and Supercharge AI Innovation
summary: Baidu has launched ERNIE 4.5 Turbo and ERNIE X1 Turbo, two new AI models offering enhanced multimodal capabilities, stronger reasoning, and lower costs. These models, along with a suite of new AI applications like Xinxiang (a multi-agent collaboration app) and highly convincing digital humans, aim to empower developers and foster AI innovation. Baidu is also promoting its AI Open Initiative and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to make its services easily accessible to developers, emphasizing that applications, not just models, will drive the future of AI.

title: Baidu: The Cheapest AI Stock In China
summary: Baidu's AI and robotaxi businesses, particularly Apollo Go and the ERNIE AI platform, are expected to drive significant growth, with AI user acquisition strategies boosting revenue by 2025. Despite geopolitical risks and potential delisting concerns, Baidu is considered undervalued, trading at a substantial discount to competitors. The company's Cloud services and the ERNIE platform show strong user adoption, and China's economic recovery is anticipated to positively impact its advertising business, making BIDU a compelling investment despite slower growth.

title: Baidu's Apollo and CAR Inc to launch autonomous car rental service
summary: Baidu's autonomous driving unit, Apollo, has formed a strategic partnership with CAR Inc, a leading car rental service provider, to launch the country's first autonomous car rental service. This collaboration aims to integrate self-driving technology into cultural tourism, accelerating the large-scale deployment of autonomous driving across various scenarios. The service will allow users to reserve autonomous vehicles for flexible travel, with the partners also working to establish industry standards for safety and reliability.

title: Baidu, Inc. (BIDU): Among Michael Burry Stocks with Huge Upside Potential
summary: Baidu, Inc. (NASDAQ:BIDU) is highlighted as one of Michael Burry's stock picks with significant upside potential, according to Scion Asset Management's Q4 2024 filings. Despite a recent analyst downgrade due to unclear AI monetization strategies and competition, Baidu's AI Cloud segment is projected for strong growth, offsetting challenges in its advertising business. The article emphasizes Burry's value-oriented, contrarian investment approach and the analytical methodology used to identify these high-potential stocks.

title: China’s Baidu Plans Robotaxi Expansion to Europe and Turkey
summary: Baidu Inc. is planning to expand its autonomous ride-hailing service, Apollo Go, to Europe and Turkey. The company is reportedly in discussions with Swiss Post unit PostAuto to launch robotaxi services in Switzerland, aiming to begin testing by the end of this year. This move marks a global expansion for Baidu's self-driving technology.

title: Baidu prepares to launch driverless taxi in Europe, WSJ reports
summary: Baidu is reportedly planning to expand its driverless ride-hailing service, Apollo Go, into Europe, with initial tests aimed for Switzerland and possible expansion into Turkey. The company intends to establish a local entity in Switzerland and begin technology testing by the end of 2025, according to the Wall Street Journal. Swiss Post, the parent company of the bus service-provider PostAuto, stated there is currently no partnership with Baidu.

title: Baidu Stock Jumps. How AI Powered the Search-Engine Provider’s Earnings Beat.
summary: Baidu's stock rose significantly after the Chinese tech company exceeded first-quarter earnings expectations. This strong performance was primarily driven by the growth of its artificial intelligence business. The article highlights how AI powered the search-engine provider's earnings beat.

title: Baidu, Inc. (NASDAQ:BIDU) is favoured by institutional owners who hold 42% of the company
summary: Institutional investors hold 42% of Baidu, Inc. (NASDAQ:BIDU), indicating their strong interest but also potential share price sensitivity to their trading. The CEO, Yanhong Li, is the largest individual shareholder with 19%, while the general public holds a 38% stake. This ownership structure suggests a company with a credible standing in the investment community and a significant insider stake.

title: China’s Baidu declares war on OpenAI and others by open-sourcing Ernie AI model
summary: Baidu has announced it will open-source its Ernie 4.5 generative AI model, signaling a shift from performance battles to a price war in the AI industry. This move challenges Western competitors like OpenAI and Anthropic by commoditizing high-performance AI and undercutting licensing fees, aiming for wider adoption and a robust developer ecosystem. Baidu's strategy builds on earlier efforts this year to offer Ernie for free and drastically reduce prices for its models, pushing pressure on competitors and potentially allowing Baidu to navigate U.S. sanctions given the global collaborative nature of open-source development.

title: Baidu’s Ernie Goes Open Source, Shaking Up the Global AI Race
